name: sdlc-workflow
description: Complete SDLC workflow as six skill-mode routers plus a setup-wide-logging command. /wf executes the canonical 13-stage lifecycle (shape, slice, plan, implement, verify, review, handoff, ship, retro) plus four perf/observability augmentations (instrument, experiment, benchmark, profile). /wf-quick starts compressed flows (quick, rca, probe, investigate, discover, hotfix, update-deps, refactor, ideate, simplify) with no-flag positional slug detection against the global registry `.ai/workflows/INDEX.md` (v9.10.0). v9.14.0 closes the 'verified but actually broken' gap with three coordinated changes: (1) `/wf verify` gains a **user-observable AC gate** that refuses `result: pass` when any user-observable acceptance criterion lacks runtime evidence — partition uses a hybrid wording heuristic with an optional `observable: true|false` AC-level override; the new `result: blocked-runtime-evidence-missing` variant distinguishes procedural fail (no evidence) from substantive fail (evidence shows AC unmet). (2) New **`/wf-quick probe <slug> [target]`** — slug-mode only runtime-truth verification that drives the running artifact, captures observable output (screenshots, stdout, responses), reads it, and writes findings as a compressed slice (`slice-type: probe`). Free-form target string resolved through four layers (AC text match, slice match, surface inference, ad-hoc) all compose; default focus mode reports incidentals, opt-in `--strict` filter mode archives them to a side file (`probe-evidence/<descriptor>/incidental.md`). `--from <path>` accepts multi-target files; `--adapter <key>` narrows to a single matched adapter; default is run-all-matched. Branch posture on mismatch asks the user (switch / run-and-record / abort) rather than auto-switching. Bootstrap is two-phase: try-to-resolve, then write an `awaiting-environment` slice with `bootstrap-failure: {step, exit-code, output-tail, remediation}` so re-running picks up where it left off. (3) New shared **runtime-adapter registry** (`skills/wf/reference/runtime-adapters.md`) keyed by platform — web, android, ios, cli, desktop, service, notebook — each declaring detection signals, bootstrap steps, drive recipe, observe recipe, teardown, evidence layout, and remediation hints. Verify's interactive sub-agent 3 and probe both read this single file; adding a new platform is a one-file change. Deferral escape hatch (`interactive-verification: deferred`) recorded under `00-index.md.runtime-evidence-deferrals[]`; `/wf review`/`/wf handoff` warn but proceed; `/wf ship` HARD-BLOCKS while any deferral has `cleared-by: null`. `/wf-meta status` parses deferrals and renders a `Runtime` column (`clean`/`deferrals: N`/`probe-findings: N`) on every dashboard table. v9.13.0 repurposed `/wf-quick discover` and `/wf-quick investigate` from product-strategy gates to engineering hypothesis-test (FOR/AGAINST/counter-hypothesis adjudication) and solution-options sketcher (2–3 distinct approaches with tradeoffs, no winner picked). v9.12.0 rewrites `/wf-meta init-ship-plan` from template-instantiation to **discovery → hypothesis → confirm**: Step 1 reads CI workflow YAML, infra-as-code (Dockerfile/helm/terraform/fly.toml/etc.), package manifests, release tooling (release-please/changesets/goreleaser/cliff), and runbook directories before any AskUserQuestion; Step 2 walks each required-core block (A–G) as a hypothesis derived from discovery with `Other (describe)` always present; Step 3 adds open-schema `additional-contracts[]` extensions (data-migration, feature-flag-rollout, infrastructure-as-code, mobile-app-store, compliance-gate, data-pipeline, schema-registry, Other) for project-specific shape; templates demoted from control-flow branches to hypothesis seeds (`--from-template <kind>` still accepted). The ship-plan frontmatter gains `template-hint` (informational) and `additional-contracts[]` (open schema) — `/wf ship` reads the required core unchanged, so no migration. `/wf-meta amend ship-plan` updated to skip discovery (existing plan is ground truth) and to route additional-contract edits via

→ freeform `id`. v9.11.0 extends INDEX.md to four more read-side surfaces: `/wf-meta status`, `/wf-meta resume`, and `/wf-meta next` enumerate from the registry instead of globbing (status still reads each `00-index.md` for Dashboard Mode's rich fields; resume/next pickers list `slug — status — updated-at` directly from INDEX.md). `/wf intake` gained registry-grounded collision detection — derived slugs that already exist in INDEX.md trigger an AskUserQuestion (resume / amend / pick-new-slug for open workflows; reopen / pick-new-slug for closed workflows; slug stability invariant prevents reuse of closed slugs). `/wf` gained a centralized Step 0.5 fuzzy-suggest in `skills/wf/SKILL.md` — unknown slug args against the 13 slug-consuming sub-commands trigger a Levenshtein-1 / substring match against INDEX.md and surface

. All four reads gracefully fall back to glob when INDEX.md is absent. The registry is a tab-separated `slug<TAB>status<TAB>workflow-type<TAB>branch<TAB>updated-at` file maintained by `/wf-meta sync` (bootstrap on first run, refresh on every run) and additively touched by every slug-mode write. /wf-meta navigates and manages existing workflows (next, status, resume, sync, amend, extend, skip, close, how, announce, init-ship-plan). /wf-design dispatches across 22 design sub-commands (shape, craft, audit, critique, plus 15 transformations and 3 setup/extract operators) in three invocation modes — workflow stage 2b (writes 02b-design.md), workflow + sub-command (writes 02c-craft.md, 07-design-audit.md, 07-design-critique.md, or design-notes/<sub>-<timestamp>.md depending on category), and freestanding (no workflow). /wf-docs runs the documentation pipeline (discover → audit → plan → generate → review) or invokes a single Diátaxis primitive — plan, tutorial, how-to, reference, explanation, readme, review — to write one document. /review runs per-dimension or named-sweep code review across 31 dimensions and 7 sweeps via parallel sub-agent dispatch. The wf-validate pre-write hook gates artifact writes against schema. Workflow-type aware: compressed mode (workflow-type: quick → reads 01-quick.md), forwarded mode (workflow-type: rca/investigate → reads 01-rca.md/01-investigate.md as rich context behind the synthesized 02-shape.md), standard mode (per-slice files). No-optional-artifacts policy enforced: every stage's Conditional Inputs row is mandatory when present. Per-slice review artifacts (07-review-<slice>.md + 07-review-<slice>-<command>.md) so reviewing a second slice never overwrites the first. wf-handoff refuses to proceed if any per-slice review has unresolved blockers. Augmentations register on 00-index.md and produce type-specific deliverables that downstream stages consume: instrument adds 04b-instrument.md signals; experiment writes 04c-experiment.md cohort/rollback design; benchmark writes 05c-benchmark.md baseline/compare; design-* writes design-notes/<sub>-<timestamp>.md and 02b-design.md / 02c-craft.md visual contracts. v9.5.0 overhauled handoff and ship: handoff stage 8 added a PR-readiness block (commitlint, public-surface drift, doc-mirror regen, PR comment triage with bounded 5-iteration loop, rebase onto base, live PR readiness check) producing readiness-verdict that ship gates on; ship stage 9 split into a project-level .ai/ship-plan.md (authored once via /wf-meta init-ship-plan with 6 templates: kotlin-maven-central, npm-public, pypi, container-image, server-deploy, library-internal) and per-release accumulating run artifacts 09-ship-run-<run-id>.md indexed by 09-ship-runs.md, replayable via 13 idempotent steps that resume from status: awaiting-input. v9.4.0 promoted /wf-docs to its own skill router and folded the 7 standalone Diátaxis skills (diataxis-doc-planner, tutorial-writer, how-to-guide-writer, reference-writer, explanation-writer, readme-writer, docs-reviewer) into it as primitive references. v9.3.0 folded /wf-design into the same skill-router shape as the other four routers (the standalone commands/wf-design.md was removed; the design skill now dispatches directly). v9.0.0-alpha.4 collapsed the 13 lifecycle slash commands into the /wf skill router; alpha.3 collapsed 10 lifecycle-navigation commands into /wf-meta; alpha.2 collapsed 10 standalone-workflow commands into /wf-quick; alpha.1 collapsed 38 review slash commands into the /review skill. Release notes for every v8.x feature live in CHANGELOG.md.
